summary: High‑energy audience recording featuring a killer Jack Straw, Bertha, Watchtower and a soaring Morning Dew
review: Fans rave about the explosive energy of this March ’91 show, especially the first‑set classics like Jack Straw and Bertha and a blistering Watchtower/Space sequence that erupts into one of the most memorable Morning Dews ever captured on tape. The audience‑sourced recording is praised as a favorite version with clear enough sound to enjoy the fireworks, though some listeners note vocal flubs and occasional miscues in the second set.

ai_rating.rationale: One reviewer gives a perfect 5‑star glowing appraisal while another rates it 0 stars, citing many flubs; averaging the strong musical moments against noticeable performance issues yields an overall middling score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Listeners describe this as their favorite audience version, with no specific complaints about clarity or mix; the tape captures the live atmosphere well.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set builds a high‑octane momentum that carries into an equally charged second set; despite several vocal and instrumental flubs, the overall pacing keeps the crowd dancing.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Described as "shredding" with fiery solo work throughout the show.
